requirements.evidence: engine/split/proposer.go defines SplitProposer interface with ProposeSplit(fileContent []byte) (SplitPlan, error) and zero gRPC/agent-transport dependency, matching acceptance criteria verbatim.

architecture_conformance.evidence: SplitPlan/SplitFileProposal/SectionRange types carry no fileID allocation, content/stub writes, or redirect bookkeeping -- consistent with orchestrate.go's documented scope boundary assigning that to issue #12. Doc comments explicitly cross-reference 2b.2.2 (mock) and issue #12 (execution), showing deliberate, non-over-designed scoping.

regression_risk.evidence: git diff 9c5baf6..02b6e2d --stat for trigger.go, guard.go, orchestrate.go and their tests: empty (byte-identical). Full go test ./split/... -race -v -count=1 -timeout 10m: all prior 2b.1.1/2b.1.2/2b.1.3 tests pass alongside new TestSplitProposerInterface.

edge_cases.evidence: ProposeSplit signature (fileContent []byte only) is minimal but sufficient for what's documented; no fileID/status param is needed since SplitProposer is stateless proposal-only per scope boundary. Minor: SectionRange has no validation of Start<=End or bounds-checking against len(fileContent) documented as an invariant, but that's plausibly deferred to the real/mock implementations (2b.2.2), not this interface definition -- acceptable for an interface-only subtask.

security.evidence: No transport, no I/O, no secrets in this interface-only diff. N/A beyond documented 'must not mutate fileContent' contract.

performance.evidence: No runtime logic added; pure type/interface declarations.

maintainability.evidence: Doc comments are thorough and explicitly delineate scope vs issue #12 and 2b.2.2, reducing future ambiguity.

test_coverage.evidence: proposer_test.go's TestSplitProposerInterface assigns fakeSplitProposer to a `var proposer SplitProposer` typed variable and calls ProposeSplit through that interface value (not the concrete type directly), genuinely exercising interface satisfaction. Covers both success path (DeepEqual on returned plan) and error path (errors.Is on wrapped error, zero-value plan returned).

confidence.evidence: go build ./..., go vet ./..., gofmt -l . all clean. go list -deps ./split/... plus grep -rniE 'grpc|protobuf|google.golang.org' split/ show zero gRPC/protobuf imports anywhere in the package's dependency graph or source (only doc-comment prose mentions gRPC).

possible_improvements.evidence: Consider documenting (not necessarily enforcing in this subtask) that SectionRange.Start/End should satisfy 0 <= Start <= End <= len(fileContent), so 2b.2.2's mock and issue #12's real consumer share a validation expectation. Non-blocking.

scope_check.evidence: git show 02b6e2d --stat: only proposer.go, proposer_test.go, and .cdr/runs/2026-07-07/010-implementation/* touched. No changes to trigger.go/guard.go/orchestrate.go or their tests.

commands_run: go test ./split/... -race -v -count=1 -timeout 10m -> all PASS, zero regressions
